checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: Accounts receivable rose from 5,831 (FY2021) to 9,027 (FY2022) to 13,143 (FY2023), i.e. +54.8% then +45.6%, while revenue grew only +16.2% then +10.9%
AccountsNotesAndLoansReceivableNetCurrent grew 7,219 (2021)->10,805 (2022)->14,943 (2023) far outpacing revenue growth
Receivables represent under 0.4% of ~$4B revenue (13,143 vs 3,969,453 in 2023), so the divergence, while present, is immaterial to a cash-based grocery model

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill moved from 63,993 (2022) to 78,556 (2023) to 78,778 (2024), roughly in line with total-asset growth from 2,772,404 to 3,173,821
OtherAssetsNoncurrent actually declined from 10,230 (2023) to 6,869 (2024) to 5,125 (2025-09-27), the opposite of a soft-asset build-up
Goodwill held flat at 747,943 for years and only rose to 782,734 in 2024 consistent with a disclosed acquisition, not internal capitalization

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: Operating cash flow consistently exceeds net income: OCF 303,447 vs NI 79,437 (FY2023) and OCF 111,963 vs NI 39,465 (FY2024)
In FY2022 OCF 185,511 exceeded NI 65,052, a healthy (cash>earnings) relationship consistent with heavy depreciation (D&A 78,251 FY2022, 108,206 FY2024)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No inventory line item was provided in the dataset, so inventory-to-sales trends cannot be directly tested; margin stability shows no COGS/revenue inconsistency

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to revenue and cash flow volatility?

evidence.quote: Operating income swung from -22,508 (Q1 2025) to 22,824 (Q3 2025), indicating earnings track underlying volatility rather than being managed to a smooth trend

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent GREW materially with the business, from 1,538 (2022) to 4,338 (2023) to 5,402 (2024) to 11,127 (2025-09-27) - conservative, not drained
Total liabilities rose steadily from 1,662,190 (2022) to 1,976,437 (2024) to 2,170,031 (2025-09-27), no sign of reserve release

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: A 10-K/A and a 10-Q/A were both filed on 2021-09-03, indicating a mid-year correction/amendment to previously filed periodic reports
No NT 10-K/NT 10-Q late-filing notifications appear and 10-K/10-Q cadence is otherwise timely and regular (e.g., 10-K filed 2025-02-26, 10-Q 2025-11-05)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Dataset spans FY2017 through Q3 FY2025 with income statement, balance sheet and cash flow concepts, enabling multi-period trend analysis
Only notable gap is the absence of an inventory line item, limiting CL4's inventory test; all other checklist items are supportable

top_signals: Receivables grew far faster than revenue in FY2022-FY2023 (AR +55% then +46% vs revenue +16% then +11%), but on immaterial absolute balances (<0.4% of sales) for a cash-based grocer
Rapidly rising doubtful-account allowance (1,538 in 2022 to 11,127 by Q3 2025) is conservative and points to collection strain rather than earnings overstatement
A 10-K/A and 10-Q/A filed same day (2021-09-03) indicate a prior-period amendment worth noting, though no restatement magnitude is evident in the data
Operating cash flow consistently exceeds net income and gross margins are stable ~30-31%, arguing against systematic earnings inflation
